brand_voice: Enthusiast-led and plain-spoken. Mr Gadget is an Australian tech review and buyer's guide publication, not a retailer — copy reads like advice from a mate who actually tested the thing in an Australian context (humid summers, cold-snap winters, 240V power, AU plug shapes, local warranty). The register is casual but informed: opinionated where the testing supports it, honest about limits, and never marketing-fluff. Avoid US-centric framing, vague superlatives ('amazing', 'game-changing'), and spec-sheet regurgitation that adds no judgement.
tone_direction: Lead with the verdict and the use-case (who it's for, where it falls short), not the specs. Reference Australian-specific context where it matters (climate, retailers, electrical standards, distance from major cities). Avoid affiliate-style hype phrasing.
usp: Australian-focused tech reviews and buyer's guides — products tested and recommended for local conditions, retailers, and warranty reality.
site_type_note: TEST CLIENT — onboarded via /ldseo-onboard skill validation on 2026-05-10. Mr Gadget is a review/content publication, not a Shopify retailer. The collection-SEO pipeline (Shopify collections → metadata → content briefs keyed off dominant_product_type) does not apply here. Editorial SEO workflows (topic clusters, content gap, review-page optimisation) would be the right fit if this were a real engagement.

folders._note: Subfolders (03_audits, 05_content, 07_reports) not created — test onboarding only.
